WARNER A TOOL


Attorney Moore intimated that
c. b. Warner, the man who Joined
the |. w. w. one day, made Is
minute talk that night which the
]. w. w. leaders did not sanction
and dropped out of sight immedi-
ately afterwards, was hired to dis
credit the organization. The man
stated that he came from Calves
ton to aid in the free speech fight.


In his 15-minute talk, in which
Perry stated that Warner talked
as fast as he could and as loud as
he could" expe1ding his strength
and vocabulary within a few min.
utes, he exhorted the fighters to
stay with the ship and ended with
a declaration that he would pull
down Cid Glory and throw it into
the ocean and raise the red flag to
the top of Pixies peak, where it
could be seen by everyone"


r. B. Carr, former reporter on
the Spokesman-Review, testified
that he had covered one of the
meetings in the municipal court
and that his story had been
changed in many ways by the city
editor, quotation being changed
to suit the taste of the boss
